entries.title: Your Act 1 deck lacked decisive damage.
entries.likelyCause: The deck took too many slow or defensive cards before solving elite and boss damage checks.
entries.whatWentWrong: Act 1 rewards decks that can end fights quickly. Without enough frontload, even solid block turns can become health loss over time.
entries.fixNextRun: Prioritize efficient attacks, early upgrades, and pathing that lets you test damage before committing to elite-heavy routes.
entries.priorityUpgrade: Upgrade a premium attack before adding more defensive tools.

entries.title: Act 2 punished unstable defensive turns.
entries.likelyCause: The deck could produce damage, but it did not block reliably against multi-enemy pressure.
entries.whatWentWrong: Act 2 asks for repeatable block, not only one strong defensive turn. Bad draw order can expose that gap fast.
entries.fixNextRun: Value block density, weak application, and card draw before taking another narrow damage payoff.
entries.priorityUpgrade: Add a consistent block engine or a draw card that finds defense on demand.

entries.title: The deck reached Act 3 without a long-fight plan.
entries.likelyCause: Your deck solved hallway fights but could not grow fast enough for boss-length combat.
entries.whatWentWrong: Act 3 bosses punish decks that only have static numbers. You need repeatable scaling or a way to end the fight quickly.
entries.fixNextRun: Identify your scaling lane earlier and stop adding cards that do not support it.
entries.priorityUpgrade: Upgrade the card that improves your scaling engine or makes it easier to draw.

entries.title: The Heart exposed draw inconsistency.
entries.likelyCause: The deck had strong turns, but it could not find the right cards under Heart pressure.
entries.whatWentWrong: High-impact cards are not enough if the deck cannot access them on the turns that matter.
entries.fixNextRun: Trim weak cards, add draw, and build around reliable stance or energy sequencing.
entries.priorityUpgrade: Take a draw or consistency upgrade over another payoff card.

entries.title: Your deck missed a core checkpoint.
entries.likelyCause: The run was short on one key axis for the fight you reached.
entries.whatWentWrong: Your deck likely solved some hallway fights, but it did not convert that strength into a reliable plan for the next pressure spike.
entries.fixNextRun: Take cards and relics that solve the upcoming Act instead of only improving the current floor.
entries.priorityUpgrade: Add one consistent source of damage, block, scaling, or draw before the next elite path.
